svg.text: AMHARIC — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
SERIES 31D OF 60 · REMEMBER FORWARD
— PASSAGE 1 — ETHIOPIAN PROVERB —
አመሳ ለካዕኙ ያስሳጣል።
əməsa lək'aNətu yasəsatəal.
The fish because-of-its-mouth is-caught.
"A fish is caught because of its mouth." — Be careful what you say.
— PASSAGE 2 — ETHIOPIAN PROVERB —
እድሞውን ዪተናገውት እንኪድም ተናገክ መዘመድ ክችል።
əndəmowənə yəttənagəwat ənkədəm tənagək məzəməd kəts'il.
Before speaking what you will say, think whether it is useful.
"Before speaking, think whether what you will say is useful."
— PASSAGE 3 — MOTTO OF ETHIOPIA —
ኢትዮጵያ ለአለም
Ityopy'a ləaləm
"Ethiopia forever" — Traditional patriotic expression.
— BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
ይህ የተሳነው መልእክት ለእረስዎ በነጳ ተዸኝነውነት ።
Yəh yətəsaNəwə mələəkt ləərəswo bənəts'ina təzəɨwənəwənt.
This message made for-you freely who-remembered future.
"This message was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN AMHARIC —
ወደዓይት (wədəayt): "future" — lit. "the ahead / the forward direction". From wəd (toward) + ayt (direction).
Amharic has no simple future tense — uses imperfective + auxiliary yəhəonal (it will be) for future.
təzəɨwənəwənt: "memory / remembrance" — from root z-w-n "to remember; to be mindful".
bənəts'ina: "freely / for free" — from nətsʼ (free / freedom) — same root as freedom.
— DECODING NOTE — FOR FUTURE READERS —
Amharic is the official language of Ethiopia (~35M native speakers, 100M+ L1+L2).
Written in the Ethiopic (Fidel) script — an abugida where each symbol = consonant + vowel order.
Consult Plate 31A for the full syllabary table and 7 vowel orders.
Amharic is a Semitic language — related to Arabic, Hebrew, and Tigrinya.
Ethiopia has one of the oldest continuous writing traditions in Africa, dating to the 4th century CE.
SERIES 31 · AMHARIC · PLATE D · TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
